theorem ctble_set_of_ctble_type {U : Type}

by
  have h1 : ctble U := h
  have h2 : ctble A := by
    -- Use the fact that the coercion from the subtype of A to U is injective
    -- and U is countable to deduce that the subtype of A is countable.
    have h3 : ctble (↥A) := by
      -- The coercion from ↥A to U is injective
      apply ctble_of_injective (fun x : ↥A => (x : U))
      -- Prove that the coercion is injective
      intro x y hxy
      -- If (x : U) = (y : U), then x = y in ↥A
      have h4 : (x : U) = (y : U) := hxy
      -- Since x and y are in A, and their values in U are equal, x = y in ↥A
      exact Subtype.ext h4
      -- Since U is countable, the subtype ↥A is also countable
    -- Since ↥A is countable, A is countable
    exact h3
  exact h2

by
  have h1 : ctble {x : U // x ∈ A} := by
    -- Since U is countable and {x : U // x ∈ A} is a subtype of U, it is countable.
    exact Set.countable_of_subset (Set.subset_univ _) h
  -- Now, {x : U // x ∈ A} is countable, and A is equivalent to it, so A is

by
  simpa [Set.countable] using h.subtype

by
  have h₁ : ctble A := by
    -- Since U is countable, there exists an injection from U to ℕ.
    -- The set A is a subset of U, so we can use the restriction of this injection to A.
    -- This restriction is an injection from A to ℕ, proving that A is countable.
    exact Set.countable.mono (fun x hx => hx) h
  exact h₁

by
  exact Set.countable.mono (by tauto) h

by
  exact inferInstance

by
  -- Use the fact that a subset of a countable type is countable
  have h₁ : ctble A := by
    -- Apply the property that any subset of a countable type is countable
    exact h.subset (by exact inferInstance)
  -- The result follows directly from the above step
  exact h₁

by
  have h_main : ctble A := by
    have h₁ : Countable A := Set.countable A h
    exact h₁
  exact h_main

by
  haveI : Cofintype U := Cofintype.ofCtble h
  exact (inferInstance : Cofintype A).ctble

by
  have h₁ : Countable A := by
    -- Use the fact that A is a subset of the universal set, which is countable because U is countable
    have h₂ : A ⊆ (Set.univ : Set U) := Set.subset_univ A
    -- The universal set is countable because U is countable
    have h₃ : Countable (Set.univ : Set U) := by
      simpa [Set.countable_coe] using h
    -- A subset of a countable set is countable
    exact Set.countable_of_subset h₂ h₃
  -- Convert the countability of A to ctble A
  exact h₁

by
  have h₁ : ctble ↑A := by
    -- Use the fact that a subset of a countable type is countable.
    exact Set.countable_of_injective (fun x : A => x.val) (fun x y hxy => by
      simpa [Subtype.ext_iff] using hxy)
  exact h₁

by
  have h₁ : ctble ↑A := by
    apply ctble.mono _ (by
      -- We need to show that `A` is a subset of `U`.
      -- Since `A : Set U`, every element of `A` is automatically in `U`.
      exact Set.subset_univ A)
    -- Now we need to show that `U` is countable.
    exact h
  exact h₁

by
  have h₁ : ctble A := by
    -- Use the fact that any subset of a countable type is countable
    have h₂ : ctble U := h
    -- The subset A of U inherits the countability from U
    exact Set.countable_of_subset (by simpa using h₂.countable) (Set.subset_univ A)
  exact h₁

by
  have h₁ : ctble A := by
    -- Since U is countable, we can use the fact that any subset of a countable set is countable.
    have h₂ : ctble U := h
    -- Use the fact that a subset of a countable set is countable.
    exact Set.countable.mono (by tauto) h₂
  exact h₁

by
  simpa using Set.countable.mono (by simp) h

by
  have h₁ : ctble A := by
    -- Use the fact that a subset of a countable set is countable
    have h₂ : ctble U := h
    -- Use the fact that A is a subset of U and U is countable to conclude that A is countable
    have h₃ : ctble A := by
      -- Use the fact that A is a subset of U and U is countable to conclude that A is countable

      -- Use the fact that U is countable to conclude that the universal set of U is countable

    -- Use the fact that any subset of a countable set is countable

    have h₄ : ctble (⊤ : Set U) := by

    -- Since A is a subset of a countable set, it is countable
    exact h₄.mono h₃

by
  have : ctble A := by
    -- Since U is countable, we can find an injection from U to ℕ.
    -- The subset A of U can then be mapped into ℕ via this injection.
    -- Because A is a subset of U, the image of A under this injection is a subset of ℕ.
    -- Subsets of ℕ are countable, so A is countable.
    have h₁ : ctble U := h
    exact Set.countable_of_subset h₁.countable A
  exact this

    trivial
  
  have h₃ : Set.Countable A := by

    -- Since U is countable, the universal set in U is countable.
    exact by simpa using h
  -- The result follows directly from the countability of the subset.
  exact h₁

by
  infer_instance

by
  have h_main : ctble A := by
    infer_instance

by

by
  have h₁ : ctble U := h
  -- Use the fact that a subset of a countable set is countable
  exact Set.countable.mono (by simp) h₁

simp
  ) h

by
  -- Since U is countable and A is a subset of U, A is also countable.
  have : ctble U := h
  exact ctble_of_injective (fun (x : A) ↦ x.val) (fun x y hxy ↦ Subtype.ext hxy)
